title: What to know about Cassia angustifolia before using it medicinally
intro_desc: Cassia angustifolia is a highly valued herb known for its extensive range of health benefits, including its ability to promote digestive health, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties that can help to mitigate oxidative stress. Cultivated in various parts of the world, Cassia angustifolia thrives in arid regions, exhibiting drought tolerance and requiring minimal maintenance. Botanically classified as a shrub belonging to the Fabaceae family, Cassia angustifolia exhibits distinctive features, including its narrow, linear leaves and clusters of yellow flowers. With a rich history of medicinal use dating back to ancient civilizations, Cassia angustifolia has been referenced in traditional Ayurvedic and Unani medicine, with mentions in the works of renowned herbalists and physicians.
